title: senna rigida var. inermis
intro: Senna rigida var. inermis, also known as Mexican senna, is a plant species that belongs to the legume family and is native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico. Traditionally, Mexican senna has been used for its medicinal properties, particularly for its anthelmintic and laxative effects, due to the presence of anthraquinone glycosides. The health benefits of Mexican senna include relief from constipation, weight loss, and treatment of intestinal worms. In herbal preparations, Mexican senna is often used as a dried leaf or root tea, or as a constituent in various herbal blends, although caution is advised due to potential side effects and interactions with certain medications.
